segments.text: Sing aloud to God, our strength! Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob!
Raise a song, and bring here the tambourine, the pleasant lyre with the harp.
Blow the trumpet at the New Moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.
For it is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
He appointed it in Joseph for a covenant, when he went out over the land of Egypt,
I heard a language that I didn’t know.
I removed his shoulder from the burden. His hands were freed from the basket.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
You called in trouble, and I delivered you. I answered you in the secret place of thunder. 
I tested you at the waters of Meribah.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hear, my people, and I will testify to you, Israel, if you would listen to me!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There shall be no strange god in you, neither shall you worship any foreign god.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
I am Yahweh, your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. 
Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But my people didn’t listen to my voice. Israel desired none of me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I let them go after the stubbornness of their hearts, that they might walk in their own counsels.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Oh that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I would soon subdue their enemies, and turn my hand against their adversaries.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The haters of Yahweh would cringe before him, and their punishment would last forever.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But he would have also fed them with the finest of the wheat. I will satisfy you with honey out of the rock.
segments.speaker: God
